observations.recommendation:
We recommended and it is agreed that Management:
1.10.1  Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify tangible items, with costs below the capitalization threshold and already issued to end-users but have remained in the PPE accounts following the suggested entries in accordance with the illustrative entries outlined in Annex C of COA Circular No. 2024-06 dated March 14, 2024:
Prior period adjustment 				xxx
Accumulated Depreciation- PPE Account		xxx
PPE Account						xxx
Strictly comply with Sections 4.17 to 4.19 of COA Circular 2022-04 regarding the proper documentation, reporting, and accountability of lost or missing semi-expendable properties.

2.9  We recommended that Management create a Disposal Committee to handle the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties of the Municipality following the modes of disposal and other necessary procedures set forth in COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-01 dated January 30, 2024.  Thereafter, coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the proper dropping of the properties from and recording of the proceeds of the sale, if any, in the books of accounts.

3.8  We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to choose the recognition method, whether at cost or revaluation model, that will be adopted and applied by the LGU on the whole class of its Land asset, record the proper carrying amount of the donated lands in the books of accounts, and provide the necessary disclosures as required in IPSAS 17.

We recommended that Management:
4.10.1 Require the Municipal Accounting Office to:
Ensure proper identification and regular and prompt adjustment of reconciling items in the BRS;
Fast track and follow up with the bank the retrieval of data relative to the unidentified credit memos.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to:
Ensure that the Schedule of Unreleased Checks prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant contains only checks which are unreleased or undelivered at the end of the year; and
Ensure that upon release of checks, check registers are duly signed by the intended recipients and the date of receipt is indicated.

8.12  We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accounting Office to:
Fast track and prioritize the reconciliation of remittance and billing files to resolve outstanding discrepancies;
Coordinate with the HRMO in updating the service records and maintaining close coordination with the GSIS by promptly submitting the required documents; and
Review / assess the roles of personnel assigned in the retrieval of documents and reconciliation thereof, and consider assigning additional staff to expedite the process.

We recommended to Management:
To submit basis and derived computation for the ₱900,000.00 aid to ABC for the Punong Barangay’s Honorarium;

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to:
Submit the BRS within the prescribed period and regularly adjust reconciling items;
Refer to the Schedule of Unreleased Checks for the preparation of the adjusting entry to restore the value of the unreleased checks to the cash account balance and set-up the appropriate liability, and make an entry the following year to reverse such adjustments.  Also, regular coordination with the Treasurer and vice versa relative to this matter and all other concerns of the agency involving the Accounting and Treasury Offices is highly encouraged; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to:
Send notices to the payees concerned on the existence of stale checks;
Ensure preparation of the Schedule of Unreleased Checks, a list of checks which are unreleased or undelivered at the end of the year, and submission thereof to the Municipal Accountant; and
Ensure that check registers are duly signed by recipients upon receipt of the check and are properly dated as to the date of receipt thereof.

We recommended that Management:
Consider designating a Property Custodian/Officer and/or establishing a General Service Office (GSO) to take charge of the proper identification of donated lands and securing all supporting documents thereof in order to expedite the process of transferring ownership of the lands to the LGU as well as the proper recognition thereof in the books;
Seek assistance from the Municipal and Provincial Assessor’s Office and/or consider availing the service/s of an independent and competent land appraiser, if necessary, to establish the current fair market value of the donated lands; and
Thereafter, require the Municipal Accountant to choose the recognition method to be adopted by the LGU and record the proper carrying amount of the donated lands in the books of accounts as required in IPSAS 17.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare reconciliation of the balances recorded under the Due to BIR account and take up necessary adjustments in the books and related records / BIR forms to ensure that all taxes withheld are properly accounted for and remitted accordingly on a timely manner and to avoid unnecessary penalties / surcharges.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare reconciliation of the balances recorded under the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts and take up necessary adjustments in the books and related records to ensure that all contributions and loan payments withheld are properly accounted for and remitted accordingly on a timely manner to prevent the recurrence of unreconciled/unremitted balances as well as the imposition of unnecessary penalties / surcharges by the concerned agencies; and
With regards to the over withheld PHILHEALTH contributions:
Refund to the employees the over deducted contributions or consider application thereof to the next remittances;
As to the government share, require the Accountant to make necessary adjustments on the corresponding expense recognized.

We recommended that Management:
Assess the composition of the BAC Secretariat staff whether it is sufficient to cater to the demands of the Office, and based on the results of the assessment, make necessary adjustments in the delegation of tasks to concerned personnel to address the delay in submission of contracts, if necessary; and
Instruct the BAC to immediately submit the above contracts and other supporting documents, and ensure thereafter the submission of contracts and all other required attachments to COA for review and evaluation within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the GPPA report contains all the data required therein and is properly and completely filed in such as but not limited to the “total cost incurred to date” and the complete list of the LGU’s completed and on-going PPAs.

We recommended that Management require the Inspection Team/Committee to inform COA about deliveries of procured items and furnish copies of the IARs within 24 hours after the acceptance by the personnel concerned so that timely inspection thereof can be conducted.

We recommended that Management adopt strict measures to regulate the grant of cash advances in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 by requiring the Municipal Accountant to: 

Strictly and regularly monitor and enforce the settlement of unliquidated cash advances within the reglementary period;

Discourage partial liquidation per cash advance voucher such that any outstanding balance shall be strictly refunded upon liquidation by enforcing the return or refund of any unused or unspent portion of the cash advance; and 

Ensure that previous cash advance is fully liquidated before granting additional cash advance.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

 In coordination   with the Municipal Assessor’s Office, carefully scrutinize and update the certified list of RPT Tax Delinquencies and provide aging thereof for proper monitoring;

 Intensify tax collection campaign to maximize collection of RPT due to the municipal government; and

 Send demand letters to the delinquent taxpayers and avail of the remedies provided under the Local Government Code of 1991 within the prescribed period to do so pursuant to the aforementioned provisions of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Closed for having been reiterated in this Audit Report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to immediately submit the lacking documents on or before March 24, 2023, otherwise, the same will be suspended in audit. Thereafter, ensure submission of infrastructure contracts together with all other required attachments to the auditor for review and evaluation.

We recommended that Management direct the GAD focal person to ensure that:

 GAD PPAs formulated and included in the GPB and subsequently undertaken and charged against the GAD budget are in line with the purpose for which GAD was created which, above all, should address gender issues in adherence to the requirements of PCW-NEDA-DILG JMC No. 2016-01; and 

 Any revisions/changes in the DILG-endorsed GPB be properly communicated in writing with and approved by the DILG pursuant to the same LMC.

We recommended that Management:

 Require the Municipal Planning and Development Council and the budget committee during the preparation of annual budget for 20% Development Fund to include only Programs/Projects/Activities which are feasible and could be accomplished within the target date of completion;

Implement the development projects programmed during the year as embodied in the Annual Investment /Development plan and ensure optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the benefit of the constituents of the Municipality; and

Ensure that all savings from completed projects and all projects which are no longer feasible and not yet implemented are considered for immediate realignment, subject to budgetary rules or reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the 20% Development Fund for re-appropriation to other development projects.

Revisit DBM Internal Audit Manual for Local Government Unit (LGU) for the possible institutionalization of the Internal Audit Unit.

Recommendations:
We recommended to Management to submit justification and basis on check payments made payable to the Chief of Police for the reimbursement of their anti-drug campaign and buy-bust operations.

We recommended to the Mayor through the Accounting Unit and General Services Office:

To assign personnel from the General Services Office as Property Officer to account and monitor Government Projects. Assigned personnel shall be responsible in preparing, maintaining and updating property cards (LLIPC, LRNPC, OPIPC, and BSPC);
Through assigned personnel from Municipal Engineering’s Office to at year end furnish a list of Billings received and inspected and the Status of Government Project Report as of year-end to the Accounting Unit for purposes of recording and recognition of completed projects; and
Through the Accounting Unit to reassess abovementioned projects and appropriately reclassify construction in progress accounts to its appropriate PPE classification which will subsequently be processed for insurance purposes and computation of depreciation expense.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Recommendation II not yet implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Mayor to:
Through assigned personnel from Municipal Engineering’s Office to at year end furnish a list of Billings received and inspected and the Status of Government Project Report as of year-end to the Accounting Unit for purposes of recording and setting up actual CIP;
Through the Accounting Unit to set up payable account and record appropriate Construction in Progress as of year-end based on reports submitted from Municipal Engineering’s Office; and
Through the Accounting unit to reassess abovementioned projects and record appropriate amount of Construction in Progress, PPE and Liability Accounts and submit Journal Entry Voucher to substantiate whereof.

We recommended to the Mayor to:
Through assigned personnel from Municipal Engineering’s Office to at year end furnish a list of Billings received and inspected and the Status of Government Project Report as of year-end to the Accounting Unit for purposes of recording and setting up actual CIP;
Through the Accounting Unit to set up payable account and record appropriate Construction in Progress as of year-end based on reports submitted from Municipal Engineering’s Office; and
Through the Accounting unit to reassess abovementioned projects and record appropriate amount of Construction in Progress, PPE and Liability Accounts and submit Journal Entry Voucher to substantiate whereof.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor through the BAC and TWG to:

Carefully and cautiously evaluate the legal, technical and financial documents submitted by bidders during preliminary evaluation and post-qualification before recommending the award of the contract; 
Strengthen post qualification procedures and activities in compliance with Rule X of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. This may include but not limited to the following, the Procurement Unit maintaining an updated database/ schedule of contractors and their track records (completed works) with the LGU which shall be readily available during conduct of every post qualification for purposes of verifying outstanding works; and
Explain in writing why the BAC and TWG shall not be held liable for passing the contractors aforementioned as eligible without thorough evaluation of the documents.

We recommended that Management:

1.	Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to require certifications from concerned department heads on the availability of savings from the concerned department heads requesting for augmentation; and 

2.	Enjoin the Local Sanggunian to review said requests and pass an appropriation ordinance authorizing the augmentation, if warranted.

We further recommended that department heads of the LGU carefully plan their budgets to ensure optimal utilization of public funds and prevent any augmentation.
observations.reason:
Section 5 of Appropriation Ordinance No. 10 s. 2018, the Ordinance Authorizing the Annual Budget of the Balingasag LGU for FY 2020 stating that: “Use of Savings and Augmentation in accordance with Section 336 of Republic Act 7160 and Art. 454 (b) of IRR of RA 7160, the Mayor and the Presiding Officer of the Sangguniang Bayan are authorized to augment item within the same class of their respective appropriations”, this means that augmentation of this two respective offices was already authorized in the said Appropriation Ordinance and therefore no need for another ordinance. | Partially Implemented

For monitoring in CY 2021, Augmentation basis needs declaration of savings.

b.	Send demand letters to the delinquent taxpayers and avail of the remedies through   levy on real property or judicial actions provided under the Local Government Code of 1991.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to take extra effort to collect immediately from the persons concerned the amount disallowed for the irregular claim of travelling expenses.  
We also recommended that the Municipality observe the proper procedure in the liquidation of cash advances for travelling expenses and ensure that the necessary supporting documents are attached.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce settlement or liquidation of cash advances thru the following measures:
a)      Withhold payment of salaries of concerned officials/employees should not be paid unless cash advances are fully settled.
b)   Demand letters should be issued to concerned officers/employees and disciplinary actions should be imposed to those with long overdue accounts. In case of failure to settle their accountabilities, cause the withholding or suspensions of their salaries.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to require certifications from concerned department heads on the availability of savings from the concerned department heads requesting for augmentation; and
Enjoin the Local Sanggunian to review said requests and pass an appropriation ordinance authorizing the augmentation, if warranted.
We further recommended that department heads of the LGU carefully plan their budgets to ensure optimal utilization of public funds and prevent any augmentation.

the Municipality stop the practice of allowing the processing of disbursement voucher/s without bearing the authorized signatures of the proper officials.
the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should ensure that the Disbursement Vouchers should be properly approved bearing the authorized signatures before processing and approving payments or issuing checks to guarantee that only valid claims will be paid pursuant to existing provisions of law.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipality issue demand letters to all concerned parties to settle all unliquidated cash advances granted pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
We also recommended that Management request for write-off in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 for those receivables who are already deceased with a copy of their death certificate and/or any other documents to support the request for write-off.

Recommendations:

appropriates funds from the General Fund totaling P2,738,486.12 and thereafter direct the Budget Officer and the Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments affecting the transactions listed above, and restitute the said amount to the 20% DF which was depleted due to improper charging thereof;
Henceforth, observe the proper utilization of the 20% DF pursuant to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. of 2017 to attain the government’s objectives.

Enjoin the Local Sanggunian to review said requests and pass an appropriation ordinance authorizing the augmentation, if warranted.
We further recommended that department heads of the LGU carefully plan their budgets to ensure optimal utilization of public funds and prevent any augmentation.

	the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should ensure that the Disbursement Vouchers should be properly approved bearing the authorized signatures before processing and approving payments or issuing checks to guarantee that only valid claims will be paid pursuant to existing provisions of law.

We recommended that the Municipality issue demand letters to all concerned parties to settle all unliquidated cash advances granted pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
We also recommended that Management request for write-off in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 for those receivables who are already deceased with a copy of their death certificate and/or any other documents to support the request for write-off.

We recommended that Management:
require the proponents to submit the lacking supporting documents in order to prove the validity and propriety of the disbursements and to avoid suspensions and disallowances.
stop granting subsequent educational researches without observing the guidelines set forth in the Department Order No. 16, s. 2017 dated March 20, 2017.

We recommended that for evaluation and monitoring purposes, management direct officials concerned to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment (LDRRMFP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the   projects and activities to be funded.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall closely monitor the daily collection remittances from all collectors. Should there be any discrepancies or leniency on the part of the Collecting Officers, she shall report to the Municipal Mayor to ensure that all collections are properly accounted and remitted intact by all collectors concerned, to prevent loss or misuse of government funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly exhaust all possible remedies for purposes of causing the immediate liquidation of transferred funds to concern government agencies. Order the return of unused monies. This could be initiated by the sending of letters to defaulting agencies requesting for submission of necessary documents proving the utilization or non-use of transferred funds. In addition, to establish accurate amount of receivables, the accounting of said accounts shall be thoroughly checked as to validity which shall start with the investigation of abnormal balances.

		As for the funds received by the Municipality from other government agencies which remained outstanding for more than 3 years, exhaustive effort should be made to locate the records and submit the needed documents to fully liquidate the money. If papers can no longer be recovered, request for write-off of accounts shall be sent to the COA Commission Proper so that adjustments in recording on the agency’s book of accounts may be effected.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer initiate the issuance of an order to be signed by the Municipal Mayor to require the concerned contractor to undertake the repair works on the identified portion of the Renovated Comfort Rooms.

We recommended that the Management must submit the original copies of the following supporting documents to establish the validity of the reports and transactions, to wit:
1. Letter Request for Suspension of Work Activities prepared by Menjer Construction dated May 20, 2018
2. Certification of Peace and Order dated August 15, 2018 issued by Hon. Elizabeth L. Macabuhay of Brgy. Kibanban
3. Time Extension Notice dated July 18, 2018 prepared by Engr. Ananias M. Altomera, Jr. and explanation as to why the approval of the notice were not signed by the Municipal Mayor and Menjer Construction
4. Time Extension Notice dated May 18, 2018 prepared by Engr. Ananias M. Altomera, Jr. and explanation as to why the approval of the notice were not signed by the Municipal Mayor and Menjer Construction

We recommended that Management submit immediately the required documents herein enumerated for review and strictly observed the provisions set forth in COA Circular 2009-001.

We recommended that Management:

a) 	Submits justification for the repeat order made.

b) 	Stop the practice of repeat order which is not in accordance with Sec. 51 of RA 9184 and GPPB Circular 06-2015.

We recommended that Management direct the Officials concerned to submit the complete documentations required above to support the disbursement for the final payment pursuant to P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We further recommended that Management should enforce strict compliance with Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 which require complete documentation before processing any government transaction, otherwise, payments thereof will be suspended or disallowed.

We recommended that Management direct the Officials concerned to submit the complete documentations required above to support the disbursement for the release of 10% retention money pursuant to P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We further recommended that Management should enforce strict compliance with Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 which require complete documentation before processing any government transaction, otherwise, payments thereof will be suspended or disallowed.

We recommended that Management direct the Officials concerned to submit the complete documentation required above to support the disbursement for the release of 10% retention money pursuant to P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We further recommended that Management should enforce strict compliance with Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 which require complete documentation before processing any government transaction, otherwise, payments thereof will be suspended or disallowed.

We recommended that Management direct the heads of the different implementing offices to fully implement projects funded out of the 20% Local Development Fund during the year in order to promptly provide the constituents the benefits due them.  

We further recommended that regular monitoring mechanisms be employed in assessing the rate of progress of project implementation in order to achieve the desired level of efficiency.

We recommended that Management instruct the heads of implementing offices to expedite the implementation of the downloaded projects to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time and to act promptly on documents and settlement of issues/constraints causing the delay of the implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a. Intensify tax collection campaign to maximize collection of RPT due to the municipal government in order to increase the delivery of basic services and facilities; and
b. Send demand letters to the delinquent taxpayers and avail of the remedies through   levy on real property or judicial actions provided under the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) be guided by the provisions of Sec. 272 of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provisions shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the municipal government.

We recommended that Management:

a. 	Strictly adhere to the provisions of Sec. 77 of R.A. 7160 dated October 10, 1991.
b. 	Submit written justification for the appointments of Mr. Jaime S. Paceňo and Ms. Eleanor M. Guibone as laborers and their specific differences of job description compared to other laborers to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the expenditures.
We recommended that Management should maintain detailed subsidiary ledger for each AO and continue to effect reconciliation of balances.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive shall require the inventory committee to conduct complete physical inventory of its property to check the existence, physical condition and accountability and ascertain the validity and correctness of the account balance in the financial statements and submit a report thereon to the Auditor concerned, in accordance with paragraph 27 of PPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to take extra effort to collect immediately from the persons concerned the amount disallowed for the irregular claim of travelling expenses.  
We also recommended that the Municipality observe the proper procedure in the liquidation of cash advances for travelling expenses and ensure that the necessary supporting documents are attached.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce settlement or liquidation of cash advances thru the following measures:
a)      Withhold payment of salaries of concerned officials/employees should not be paid unless cash advances are fully settled.
b)   Demand letters should be issued to concerned officers/employees and disciplinary actions should be imposed to those with long overdue accounts. In case of failure to settle their accountabilities, cause the withholding or suspensions of their salaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor discontinue drawing cash advance and procure medicines through reimbursement basis in compliance with the provisions of RA 9184.

Further, a proper procurement planning should be done to avoid incurrence of the same deficiency.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall closely monitor the daily collection remittances from all collectors. Should there be any discrepancies or leniency on the part of the Collecting Officers, she shall report to the Municipal Mayor to ensure that all collections are properly accounted and remitted intact by all collectors concerned, to prevent loss or misuse of government funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly exhaust all possible remedies for purposes of causing the immediate liquidation of transferred funds to concern government agencies. Order the return of unused monies. This could be initiated by the sending of letters to defaulting agencies requesting for submission of necessary documents proving the utilization or non-use of transferred funds. In addition, to establish accurate amount of receivables, the accounting of said accounts shall be thoroughly checked as to validity which shall start with the investigation of abnormal balances.

		As for the funds received by the Municipality from other government agencies which remained outstanding for more than 3 years, exhaustive effort should be made to locate the records and submit the needed documents to fully liquidate the money. If papers can no longer be recovered, request for write-off of accounts shall be sent to the COA Commission Proper so that adjustments in recording on the agency’s book of accounts may be effected.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer initiate the issuance of an order to be signed by the Municipal Mayor to require the concerned contractor to undertake the repair works on the identified portion of the Renovated Comfort Rooms.

We recommended that the Management must submit the original copies of the following supporting documents to establish the validity of the reports and transactions, to wit:
1.	Letter Request for Suspension of Work Activities prepared by Menjer Construction dated May 20, 2018
2. 	Certification of Peace and Order dated August 15, 2018 issued by Hon. Elizabeth L. Macabuhay of Brgy. Kibanban
3.	Time Extension Notice dated July 18, 2018 prepared by Engr. Ananias M. Altomera, Jr. and explanation as to why the approval of the notice were not signed by the Municipal Mayor and Menjer Construction
4.	Time Extension Notice dated May 18, 2018 prepared by Engr. Ananias M. Altomera, Jr. and explanation as to why the approval of the notice were not signed by the Municipal Mayor and Menjer Construction
5.	Work Suspension Order No. 5 dated May 20, 2018 prepared by Engr. Ananias M. Altomera, Jr. and explanation as to why the approval of the notice were not signed by the Municipal Mayor and Menjer Construction
6.	Work Resumption Order No. 5 dated July 17, 2018 prepared by Engr. Ananias M. Altomera, Jr. and explanation as to why the approval of the notice were not signed by the Municipal Mayor and Menjer Construction
7.	Work Resumption Order No. 4 dated May 18, 2018 prepared by Engr. Ananias M. Altomera, Jr. and explanation as to why the approval of the notice were not signed by the Municipal Mayor and Menjer Construction
8.	Work Suspension Order No. 4 dated December 29, 2017 prepared by Engr. Ananias M. Altomera, Jr. and explanation as to why the approval of the notice were not signed by the Municipal Mayor and Menjer Construction
9.	Report of Inspection and Certificate of Acceptance and explanation as to why the issued certificate was not signed by the Municipal Mayor
10.	Statement of Work Accomplishment as of July 24, 2018 prepared by Engr. Ananias M. Altomera, Jr.
11.	Statement of Work Accomplishment as of July 24, 2018 prepared by the Contractor

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before processing and approving payments or issuing checks to guarantee that only valid claims will be paid pursuant to existing provisions of law.

observations.reason: As of CY 2018, fully paid already.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce settlement or liquidation of cash advances thru the following measures:
a)      Withhold payment of salaries of concerned officials/employees should not be paid unless cash advances are fully settled.
b)   Demand letters should be issued to concerned officers/employees and disciplinary actions should be imposed to those with long overdue accounts. In case of failure to settle their accountabilities, cause the withholding or suspensions of their salaries.

Require the concerned accountable officers to restitute immediately the full amount of misappropriated fund, otherwise charges for malversation of public funds will be filed against them with the corresponding administrative action.

Require the Municipal Treasurer, who has direct supervision over the collectors to strictly implement the applicable rules and regulations to safeguard the assets of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor discontinue drawing cash advance and procure medicines through reimbursement basis in compliance with the provisions of RA 9184.

Further, a proper procurement planning should be done to avoid incurrence of the same deficiency.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management should maintain detailed subsidiary ledger for each AO and continue to effect reconciliation of balances.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to uphold immediate compliance.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the downloaded fund should be accounted separately as this carries the nature as Trust. We likewise recommended the Municipality to revisit the Section 313 of the same provision as it enumerates those special accounts that can be included in the General Fund.
We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to restitute any amount to General Fund if in case the said fund was unconsciously depleted by those expenditures paid or charged.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive shall require the inventory committee to conduct complete physical inventory of its property to check the existence, physical condition and accountability and ascertain the validity and correctness of the account balance in the financial statements and submit a report thereon to the Auditor concerned, in accordance with paragraph 27 of PPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to take extra effort to collect immediately from the persons concerned the amount disallowed for the irregular claim of travelling expenses.  We also recommended that the Municipality observe the proper procedure in the liquidation of cash advances for travelling expenses and ensure that the necessary supporting documents are attached.

We recommended that Management require Ms. Evelyn Ogatorestitute immediately the balance of the misappropriated amount, otherwise, file the appropriate charges against the accountable officer, if warranted.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce settlement or liquidation of cash advances thru the following measures:
a)      Withhold payment of salaries of concerned officials/employees should not be paid unless cash advances are fully settled.
b)   Demand letters should be issued to concerned officers/employees and disciplinary actions should be imposed to those with long overdue accounts. In case of failure to settle their accountabilities, cause the withholding or suspensions of their salaries.

We recommended that the Municipality should avoid designating an employee who has previous history of incurring cash shortages from his accountability.

We also recommended that all Accountable Officers should maintain cash books as required by the same circular and that reflect all his collections and deposits for a certain periods of time.

We recommended that Management direct the officials concerned to use the perpetual inventory method in recording supplies and materials, drugs and medicines and comply with the provisions as provided in the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I for LGUs and/or the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).

We recommended that management stop the charging of those expenditures already provided in the Deped budget to the SEF of the Municipality and observe strictly the rules and regulations in the utilization of SEF as provided in the DECS-DBM-DILG JC No.1.

We recommended that Management should submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five (5) days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We recommended that for evaluation and monitoring purposes, the concerned Agency shall prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the   projects and activities to be funded.

Likewise, we recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA Auditor through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council.

Require the concerned accountable officers to restitute immediately the full amount of misappropriated fund, otherwise charges for malversation of public funds will be filed against them with the corresponding administrative action.

Require the Municipal Treasurer, who has direct supervision over the collectors to strictly implement the applicable rules and regulations to safeguard the assets of the Municipality.
observations.reason: All salaries and emoluments of the accountable officer had been withheld and applied to her shortage. As of December 31, 2016, accountable officer’s shortage has a balance of P297,406.10.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a.	Demand from the officers and employees concerned for the immediate liquidation/settlement of their unliquidated cash advances through issuance of demand letters issued by the Municipal Accountant and noted by the Local Chief Executive, copy furnished the Audit Team. In case of non-compliance, direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of their salaries, or any money due to them.

b.	Require prompt submission to the Office of the COA Chairman the liquidation vouchers and supporting documents of Intelligence Fund;

c.	Send final demand letters to persons who have retired or out of service who still have unliquidated cash advances; and

d.	Comply strictly with the guidelines on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances provided in COA Circular No. 97-002 and require the Municipal Accountant to monitor compliance thereto to prevent the accumulation of cash advances.
observations.reason: Letters have been sent to remind the employees and officers to liquidate their cash advances. Some have resorted to payroll deduction.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records on RPT and SET receivables and make the necessary adjustments on the discrepancies,  so that a reliable receivable account would be reflected in the financial statements.

We also recommended to require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, to provide basis in setting up receivables at the beginning of the year, thereby ensuring the requirement of  the provisions of Section 20, NGAS, Volume I, for LGUs.

We recommended that for evaluation and monitoring purposes, the concerned Agency shall prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the   projects and activities to be funded.

Likewise, we recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA Auditor through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council.
observations.reason: Not Implemented | Pro-forma of reports were not secured. Management committed to comply starting CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to take extra effort to collect immediately from the persons concerned the amount disallowed for the irregular claim of travelling expenses.
We also recommended that the Municipality observe the proper procedure in the liquidation of cash advances for travelling expenses and ensure that the necessary supporting documents are attached.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Republic Act No. 9184 and its Revised  Implementing Rules and Regulation and make sure that all documents are properly signed and dated.

We recommended that Management should instruct the General Services Officer to furnish copies of Acceptance and Inspection Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four hours from acceptance to enable them to conduct timely validation thereof pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.

We recommended that Management require Ms. Evelyn Oga to restitute immediately the balance of the misappropriated amount, otherwise, file the appropriate charges against the accountable officer, if warranted.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce settlement or liquidation of cash advances thru the following measures:
a)      Withhold payment of salaries of concerned officials/employees should not be paid unless cash advances are fully settled.
b)   Demand letters should be issued to concerned officers/employees and disciplinary actions should be imposed to those with long overdue accounts. In case of failure to settle their accountabilities, cause the withholding or suspensions of their salaries.

We recommended that the Municipality should avoid designating an employee who has previous history of incurring cash shortages from his accountability.
We also recommended that all Accountable Officers should maintain cash books as required by the same circular and that reflect all his collections and deposits for a certain periods of time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the designated DRRM Officer to prioritize the preparation and submission of the LDRRMFIP, submit immediately the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization, and the maintenance of public awareness through bulletin board.  The submission and compliance of these reports has basic importance on the lives of the constituents as it promotes disaster mitigation, prevention, and preparedness. In addition, the Designated DRRM Officer must consider the prompt fulfillment of the activities or tasks enumerated in the LDRRMFIP as the most effective way of handling disaster goes with the idea that outweighs preventing over curing.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor discontinue drawing cash advance and procure medicines through reimbursement basis in compliance with the provisions of RA 9184. Further, a proper procurement planning should be done to avoid incurrence of the same deficiency.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive shall require the inventory committee to conduct complete physical inventory of its property to check the existence, physical condition and accountability and ascertain the validity and correctness of the account balance in the financial statements and submit a report thereon to the Auditor concerned, in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume I.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer and Accountant shall maintain and update their property cards and equipment ledger cards, respectively, to facilitate the inventory taking and reconciliation between the property and accounting records. Likewise, Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) should be issued to officials and employees to pinpoint accountability and responsibility.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented | The management was able to provide ledgers which were just currently bought by the agency.

Only few were made and presented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the officials concerned to use the perpetual inventory method in recording supplies and materials, drugs and medicines and comply with the provisions as provided in the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I for LGUs and/or the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | The management were able to test for a month but they admitted that it would entail a lot of time. Moreover, management assured that they will carefully study the implementation of this method.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the transactions of the agency to their proper accounts and refrain from using other maintenance and other operating expenses account if the expense can be classified to a specific account.

We recommended for appropriate adjustment to be taken up in the books of the agency using the following proposed adjusting journal entries:
	To set up the Trust Liability:
Subsidy from other NGAs (Dr.)
	Due to other NGAs	(Cr.)			xxx
To adjust expense account:
	Due to other NGAs	(Dr.)
			Other MOOE			(Cr.)

	We also recommended that the agency follow the proper accounting and reporting of funds received from other National Government Agencies as provided by the above-cited provisions and circular.

We recommended that the Municipal  Treasurer faithfully monitor and ensure compliance with Section 69 of P.D. 1445, COA Circular 92-382 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. We also recommended that administrative sanctions be imposed on all accountable officers who repeatedly did not perform their sworn duties in accordance with law and regulations.

We recommended that management stop the charging of those expenditures already provided in the Deped budget to the SEF of the Municipality and observe strictly the rules and regulations in the utilization of SEF as provided in the DECS-DBM-DILG JC No.1.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | The management reasoned out that their services were necessary but were not paid by DepEd. Nevertheless, this memorandum was further relaxed for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five (5) days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | Change of administration and change of responsible persons were the reason for the non-submission of contracts for review, POs and notices. However, with the new administration, they committed to comply with this audit observation memorandum.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly observe Section 7, Rule II, of RA 9184, andCOA Circular No. 76-41 as well as the imposition of control measures to ensure that procurement is effected in an efficient, economical and effective manner that is most advantageous to the government in order to prevent possible disallowances forsuch violation.
We recommended that the agency strictly follow the above-cited instructions on the preparation and accomplishment of the Report of Checks Issued.
